recipes.title: Saltah
recipes.description: Yemen's national dish: bubbling stew of meat and vegetables in a clay bowl, finished with frothy whipped fenugreek hilbeh.
steps: Soak fenugreek seeds in water for at least 4 hours, then drain and whip vigorously with a fork until pale and frothy.
Blend the whipped fenugreek with cilantro, garlic, green chili, salt, and a tablespoon of cold water into hilbeh.
Brown cubed lamb in oil with chopped onion until caramelized.
Stir in tomato paste, cumin, and coriander; cook 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes, salt, and 1 liter water; simmer 1 hour until lamb is tender.
Add cubed potato, carrot, and courgette; cook 20 minutes more.
Transfer the stew to individual clay bowls and bring to a hard boil over the flame.
Spoon the hilbeh foam over the bubbling stew at the table and serve immediately with bread.

recipes.title: Mandi
recipes.description: Yemeni smoky rice and chicken or lamb cooked together with cardamom and dried lime, finished with charcoal smoke.
steps: Rub lamb with cardamom, salt, and oil; rest 1 hour.
Place lamb on a rack inside a deep covered tray with 500 ml water and dried limes.
Roast at 160C for 3 hours until very tender.
Light a piece of charcoal until red, place in a heatproof bowl in the tray, drizzle with oil, and seal foil 5 minutes; remove.
Soak rice 30 minutes; bloom saffron in 3 tablespoons hot water.
Bring 1 liter of the lamb cooking juices to a boil with ghee and salt; add drained rice and saffron.
Simmer covered on lowest heat for 20 minutes until rice is tender.
Plate the rice and arrange the smoked lamb on top with toasted almonds and raisins.

recipes.title: Fahsa
recipes.description: Yemeni clay-pot bubbling stew of shredded lamb in spiced broth, lifted with a final swirl of fenugreek hilbeh.
steps: Soak fenugreek seeds in water for 4 hours, then drain and whip with a fork until frothy.
Blend whipped fenugreek with cilantro, garlic, chili, and salt to make hilbeh.
Brown cubed lamb in oil with chopped onion until deep golden.
Stir in tomato paste, cumin, coriander, and cardamom.
Add chopped tomatoes, salt, and 1 liter water; simmer 1.5 hours until very tender.
Shred the lamb in the pot using two forks.
Pour the bubbling stew into clay bowls and place over the flame until furiously boiling.
Spoon the hilbeh foam over the top at the table; serve with malooga bread.

recipes.title: Bint al-Sahn
recipes.description: Yemeni honey cake of paper-thin layered dough soaked in butter, baked golden, and drizzled with honey and nigella.
steps: Whisk eggs, yeast, salt, and warm water; mix into the flour and knead 10 minutes into a smooth elastic dough.
Rest the dough covered for 30 minutes.
Divide into 16 small balls and oil generously with ghee; rest 20 minutes.
Stretch each ball paper-thin with oiled hands.
Layer 4 sheets in an oiled round tray, brushing each with melted ghee, to make one cake.
Repeat to make 4 stacked cakes in the tray.
Bake at 200C for 20 minutes until deep golden and crisp.
Drizzle hot cakes with melted ghee and honey, then sprinkle with nigella seeds before serving.

recipes.title: Aseed
recipes.description: Yemeni stiff wheat flour dough served as a savory mound, scooped with meat broth and topped with hilbeh or honey.
steps: Bring water and salt to a rolling boil in a heavy pot.
Pour in the flour all at once and immediately beat hard with a sturdy wooden spoon.
Continue beating over low heat for 15 minutes until very thick, glossy, and pulling away from the pan.
Wet hands and shape into a smooth mound on a serving plate.
Make a well in the top with the back of a spoon.
Pour hot lamb broth and melted ghee into the well, or honey for the sweet version.
Pull pieces from the mound and dip into the broth or honey to eat.

recipes.title: Shafut
recipes.description: Cool Yemeni summer dish of layered flatbread soaked in spiced buttermilk with mint, garlic, and chili.
steps: Blend buttermilk and yogurt with chopped mint, cilantro, garlic, chili, and salt until pale green.
Tear lahoh into rough pieces and arrange in the bottom of a wide bowl.
Pour the green buttermilk over the bread and let soak 5 minutes.
Top with sliced spring onions and extra mint.
Chill thoroughly before serving.

recipes.title: Zurbian
recipes.description: Yemeni biryani-style layered rice with marinated lamb, saffron, and crispy fried potatoes; festival celebration food.
steps: Marinate lamb with yogurt, garlic, turmeric, cardamom, cinnamon, and salt for 4 hours.
Bloom saffron in 3 tablespoons hot water.
Fry sliced potatoes in oil until golden; drain.
Caramelize sliced onions in the same oil for 25 minutes until deep brown.
Cook the marinated lamb with chopped tomatoes and 500 ml water for 1.5 hours until tender.
Boil rice in salted water for 6 minutes until al dente; drain.
Layer rice, lamb, fried potato, and onions in a heavy pot; drizzle saffron water over the top.
Cover tightly and dum cook on lowest heat for 30 minutes; rest 10 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Salata Hadhramautia
recipes.description: Yemeni Hadhrami salad of tomato, cucumber, and onion in a tangy yogurt and lemon dressing with fresh herbs.
steps: Dice tomatoes, cucumber, and onion finely.
Whisk yogurt, lemon juice, grated garlic, olive oil, and salt into a dressing.
Toss the diced vegetables with chopped cilantro and mint.
Pour over the dressing and stir gently.
Chill 15 minutes and serve cold with grilled meat or rice.

recipes.title: Bajiya
recipes.description: Yemeni-Hadhrami spiced lentil and onion fritters, deep fried into crispy savory snacks for Ramadan iftar.
steps: Soak yellow split peas overnight, then drain.
Pulse split peas with chopped onion, garlic, chili, cilantro, cumin, coriander, baking soda, and salt to a coarse paste.
Rest the mixture for 15 minutes.
Heat oil to 180C.
Drop tablespoon-size scoops of the batter into the oil.
Fry for 4 minutes until deep golden and crisp.
Drain on paper and serve hot with sahawiq chili sauce.
